capabilities.name: Git Forge
capabilities.summary: Host the canonical repository, issues, pull requests, and webhook-driven automation without coupling other services to one forge vendor.
required_outcomes: Provide the authoritative git remote for platform code and automation artifacts.
Support governed webhook and CI entrypoints that other services can consume through stable repo metadata.
service_guarantees: Repository clone, push, pull request, and webhook workflows remain available through stable git and HTTP interfaces.
Repository-level auth, branch protection, and automation tokens stay manageable through repo-documented procedures.

canonical_inputs.name: git_repository_event
canonical_inputs.description: Repo-managed commits, branches, pull requests, tags, and webhook triggers emitted by platform repositories.

canonical_inputs.name: automation_runner_registration
canonical_inputs.description: Runner and webhook credentials required by downstream CI and promotion workflows.

canonical_outputs.name: git_remote_surface
canonical_outputs.description: Stable git-over-SSH or git-over-HTTPS endpoints consumed by operators, runners, and automation.

canonical_outputs.name: webhook_delivery_events
canonical_outputs.description: Repository events delivered to approved downstream automation consumers without forge-specific rewrites.

security_expectations: Support repo-managed admin bootstrap, token rotation, and named non-root automation identities.
Support SSO-backed operator login and scoped automation credentials.
audit_expectations: Repository mutations and automation token creation remain attributable to a named actor.
Webhook and runner configuration changes are exportable for review.
observability_requirements: Expose health signals for the forge UI and git transport path.
Provide logs or metrics for webhook failures and runner registration drift.
portability_constraints: Downstream automation must depend on git, webhooks, and documented API contracts rather than a Gitea-only SDK.
Repository metadata, hooks, and runner configuration must stay representable in repo-managed exports.

import_requirements: A replacement forge can import repositories and recreate webhook plus runner configuration from repo-managed artifacts.
Git remote URLs and webhook payload handling stay compatible with downstream automation contracts.
migration_expectations.fallback_behaviour: Git fetch and push access may fail closed during migration, but repository data and webhook contracts remain exportable from committed artifacts.

failure_modes.acceptable_degradation: CLI git operations may continue over SSH while browser-based repository administration is unavailable.
failure_modes.operator_response: Use the documented git and admin recovery paths rather than ad hoc UI edits on a replacement system.

current_selection.notes: Selected for self-hosted git, repo bootstrap, webhook control, and lightweight runner coordination.

capabilities.name: Identity Provider
capabilities.summary: Authenticate operators, browsers, and automation clients through one governed SSO contract that downstream services can consume without provider-specific coupling.
required_outcomes: Authenticate human and machine identities through one repo-managed OIDC boundary.
Issue stable claims consumed by oauth2-proxy, the API gateway, and operator-facing services.
service_guarantees: OIDC discovery, authorization, token, and JWKS endpoints stay stable for downstream consumers.
Realm, client, and claim mappings remain reproducible from repo-managed inputs.

canonical_inputs.name: browser_oidc_login
canonical_inputs.description: Interactive sign-in request initiated by oauth2-proxy or a browser-based operator surface.

canonical_inputs.name: machine_client_definition
canonical_inputs.description: Repo-managed OIDC client registration, scopes, redirect URIs, and secret material for automation consumers.

canonical_outputs.name: id_token
canonical_outputs.description: OIDC identity token with repo-governed identity, role, and group claims.

canonical_outputs.name: session_claims
canonical_outputs.description: Stable authorization assertions passed to downstream auth boundaries.

security_expectations: Support MFA for operator logins and short-lived browser sessions.
Support repo-managed client secret rotation and deterministic claim mapping.
audit_expectations: Successful and failed sign-in events are exportable for audit review.
Client and realm changes remain attributable to a named operator or automation identity.
observability_requirements: Expose a health signal or synthetic check for the identity control plane.
Emit latency or failure data for discovery, authorization, and token exchange paths.
portability_constraints: Downstream services consume OIDC and OAuth contracts rather than Keycloak-only SDK types.
Realm, client, and group data remain representable in portable repo-managed exports.

import_requirements: A replacement identity provider can recreate clients, groups, and claim mappings from repo-managed artifacts.
The replacement can publish OIDC discovery and JWKS endpoints without changing downstream contracts.
migration_expectations.fallback_behaviour: Existing sessions may continue until expiry, but new browser logins fail closed if the provider is unavailable.

failure_modes.acceptable_degradation: Cached sessions and recently-fetched JWKS material may continue briefly, but new interactive logins fail closed.
failure_modes.operator_response: Use documented break-glass identities only for recovery while keeping downstream services on the OIDC contract.

current_selection.notes: Selected for repo-managed OIDC, MFA, and group-aware operator login across shared edge and private surfaces.

capabilities.name: Private Access Mesh Control Plane
capabilities.summary: Coordinate zero-trust node enrollment, private routing, and operator access policy without forcing downstream automation to depend on one mesh vendor API.
required_outcomes: Provide private operator access to the Proxmox host and guest network without public SSH exposure.
Publish governed subnet routes and node identity state for repo-managed automation.
service_guarantees: Node enrollment, route advertisement, and ACL policy remain deterministic from repo-managed inputs.
Private reachability to the host and guest network remains available without exposing new public management surfaces.

canonical_inputs.name: node_enrollment_request
canonical_inputs.description: Operator or host request to enroll a node into the private mesh with a named identity.

canonical_inputs.name: route_and_acl_policy
canonical_inputs.description: Repo-managed subnet route and access control declarations for the managed mesh.

canonical_outputs.name: node_identity_record
canonical_outputs.description: Stable node identity, ownership, and route publication state for operators and automation.

canonical_outputs.name: private_management_path
canonical_outputs.description: Reachable private network path used by SSH, Ansible, and internal service verification.

security_expectations: Support named node ownership, non-public management access, and deterministic ACL policy.
Support revocation or removal of stale nodes without manual hidden state.
audit_expectations: Node enrollment and ACL changes are attributable to a named operator or automation identity.
Approved subnet route publications are exportable for review.
observability_requirements: Expose health or control-plane reachability checks for the mesh coordinator.
Surface connected-node and subnet-route state for verification.
portability_constraints: Downstream automation depends on private IP reachability and identity metadata rather than Headscale-specific APIs.
ACLs, route ownership, and enrollment policy remain representable in repo-managed exports.

import_requirements: A replacement mesh control plane can recreate node ownership, ACLs, and subnet routes from repo-managed artifacts.
The replacement preserves the private management path to the host and guest network.
migration_expectations.fallback_behaviour: Existing private sessions may continue temporarily, but new enrollments or route changes pause until the replacement mesh control plane is ready.

failure_modes.acceptable_degradation: Already-connected nodes may retain reachability for a time, but new enrollments and policy changes stop.
failure_modes.operator_response: Recover the control plane before widening public management access; preserve the private-only operator model.

current_selection.notes: Selected for self-hosted mesh coordination and controlled subnet routing into the private guest network.

capabilities.name: Secret Authority
capabilities.summary: Store, issue, and rotate runtime secrets through one governed contract that keeps downstream workloads independent of a single secret manager API.
required_outcomes: Provide the authoritative secret store for runtime credentials, tokens, and bootstrap material.
Support repo-managed secret distribution and rotation without committed plaintext secrets.
service_guarantees: KV paths, auth methods, and lease-backed injection workflows remain reproducible from repo-managed automation.
Secret consumers can receive rendered runtime material without embedding OpenBao-only logic.

canonical_inputs.name: secret_definition
canonical_inputs.description: Repo-managed secret identifier, rotation metadata, and delivery contract for a runtime credential.

canonical_inputs.name: consumer_auth_binding
canonical_inputs.description: Named workload or automation identity authorized to read or render a governed secret path.

canonical_outputs.name: versioned_secret_material
canonical_outputs.description: Rendered secret value or payload delivered to the approved workload or operator surface.

canonical_outputs.name: rotation_metadata
canonical_outputs.description: Lease, age, and rotation policy information used by secret automation.

security_expectations: Support mTLS or equivalent strong auth for private secret delivery paths.
Support named workload auth policies and governed rotation workflows.
audit_expectations: Secret writes, rotations, and policy changes are attributable to named identities.
Auth and path policies remain exportable for review.
observability_requirements: Expose seal state, auth health, and request failure signals for the secret authority.
Surface lease or rotation drift through repo-managed reporting.
portability_constraints: Consumers rely on injected files or environment contracts rather than OpenBao-specific client code.
Secret metadata, policies, and path layout remain representable in portable repo-managed exports.

import_requirements: A replacement secret authority can recreate auth policies, secret metadata, and path layout from repo-managed artifacts.
Consumers can continue using injected runtime files or env contracts without changing application code.
migration_expectations.fallback_behaviour: Existing rendered secrets may remain usable until rotated, but new reads and rotations pause until the replacement authority is available.

failure_modes.acceptable_degradation: Running workloads may continue on already-rendered secrets, but new secret reads and rotations fail closed.
failure_modes.operator_response: Recover the secret authority rather than hand-writing replacement secrets outside the documented contract.

current_selection.notes: Selected for repo-managed secret policy, private mTLS publication, and controlled runtime injection.

capabilities.name: Service Inventory And Topology
capabilities.summary: Keep the canonical inventory of hosts, IPs, prefixes, and service ownership in a replaceable topology contract rather than a product-specific data model.
required_outcomes: Provide the authoritative record of managed hosts, VMs, prefixes, and service ownership.
Support repo-managed synchronization into a browsable operator inventory.
service_guarantees: Managed prefixes, IP assignments, device records, and service ownership remain reproducible from repo-managed inputs.
Topology data can be queried without coupling consumers to one inventory vendor schema.

canonical_inputs.name: topology_snapshot
canonical_inputs.description: Repo-managed host, VM, prefix, IP, and service declarations for the current platform estate.

canonical_inputs.name: synchronization_run
canonical_inputs.description: Governed sync request that applies the canonical repo view to the live inventory system.

canonical_outputs.name: inventory_records
canonical_outputs.description: Queryable host, VM, IPAM, and ownership records aligned with the repo-managed topology.

canonical_outputs.name: sync_drift_signal
canonical_outputs.description: Evidence that the live inventory still matches or diverges from repo-managed topology.

security_expectations: Support scoped automation credentials and read-only operator views where appropriate.
Keep authoritative topology edits in repo-managed automation rather than manual hidden UI state.
audit_expectations: Topology mutations are attributable to a named operator or automation identity.
Exported inventory objects remain reviewable against repo-managed topology.
observability_requirements: Expose inventory reachability or API health for sync verification.
Surface sync drift or failed object reconciliation.
portability_constraints: Downstream consumers depend on canonical service and topology terms rather than NetBox-only field names.
Inventory exports remain transformable into another IPAM or inventory system.

import_requirements: A replacement inventory system can import the canonical topology and ownership artifacts from repo-managed data.
Synchronization logic can recreate stable service, VM, and network objects without NetBox-specific identifiers.
migration_expectations.fallback_behaviour: The repo remains the canonical topology source while live inventory sync is paused or being migrated.

failure_modes.acceptable_degradation: Operators lose the browsable inventory UI, but repo-managed topology and sync inputs remain intact.
failure_modes.operator_response: Use the repo as the source of truth while repairing or replacing the live inventory surface.

current_selection.notes: Selected for operator-facing topology browsing while the repo stays canonical for managed inventory inputs.

capabilities.name: Workflow Orchestrator
capabilities.summary: Run governed operator and automation workflows behind a stable execution contract so later product changes stay bounded by adapters and workflow metadata.
required_outcomes: Execute repo-managed workflows with approval, audit, and retry semantics.
Expose a durable execution surface for operators, agents, and scheduled automation.
service_guarantees: Named workflows, schedules, and parameters remain reproducible from repo-managed sources.
Execution metadata, logs, and result handling remain stable for operators and dependent services.

canonical_inputs.name: workflow_definition
canonical_inputs.description: Repo-managed workflow code, metadata, schedules, and approval requirements.

canonical_inputs.name: workflow_invocation
canonical_inputs.description: Operator or automation request to execute one governed workflow with typed parameters.

canonical_outputs.name: workflow_run_record
canonical_outputs.description: Durable execution status, logs, and result metadata for one workflow run.

canonical_outputs.name: workflow_schedule_state
canonical_outputs.description: Current enabled or disabled schedule posture for governed recurring workflows.

security_expectations: Support named operator and automation identities with scoped execution rights.
Keep approvals, tokens, and schedule changes inside repo-documented control paths.
audit_expectations: Workflow launches, approvals, and schedule edits remain attributable to named actors.
Execution history is exportable for incident or change review.
observability_requirements: Expose workflow health, queue, and execution failure signals.
Provide durable logs or traces for governed workflow runs.
portability_constraints: Workflow callers depend on the repo-managed workflow catalog and HTTP or CLI boundaries rather than Windmill-only types.
Workflow code and metadata remain exportable as source files and manifests.

import_requirements: A replacement orchestrator can recreate workflow code, schedules, and approval metadata from repo-managed artifacts.
Operators can still trigger workflows through named repo entrypoints during or after migration.
migration_expectations.fallback_behaviour: Paused scheduling is acceptable during migration as long as manual repo-triggered recovery paths remain available.

failure_modes.acceptable_degradation: Scheduled and browser-triggered workflows pause, but controller-local repo entrypoints remain available for recovery.
failure_modes.operator_response: Use documented controller-side entrypoints for high-priority recovery while restoring the orchestrator.

current_selection.notes: Selected for governed workflow execution, schedules, and API-backed operator automation.
